chronology: The arrangement of events or dates in the order of their occurrence.
evidence: The available body of facts or information indicating whether a belief or proposition is true or valid.
archaeology: The study of human history and prehistory through the excavation of sites and the analysis of artefacts and other physical remains.
historian: An expert in or student of history, especially that of a particular period, geographical region, or social phenomenon.
source: A place, person, or thing from which something originates or can be obtained.
timeline: A graphical representation of a period of time, on which important events are marked.
modern: Relating to the present or recent times as opposed to the remote past.
ancient: Belonging to the very distant past and no longer in existence.
archaeologist: A person who studies human history and prehistory through the excavation of sites and the analysis of artefacts and other physical remains.
history: The study of past events, particularly in human affairs.
The whole series of past events connected with a particular person or thing.
A continuous, typically chronological, record of important or public events or of a particular trend or institution.
A record kept by a web browser of the web pages and other files it has been used to access.
prehistory: the period of time before written records.
